2604.13940.title: AI-Assisted Peer Review at Scale: The AAAI-26 AI Review Pilot
2604.13940.paper_summary: Scientific peer review faces mounting strain as submission volumes surge, making it increasingly difficult to sustain review quality, consistency, and timeliness. Recent advances in AI have led the community to consider its use in peer review, yet a key unresolved question is whether AI can generate technically sound reviews at real-world conference scale. Here we report the first large-scale field deployment of AI-assisted peer review: every main-track submission at AAAI-26 received one clearly identified AI review from a state-of-the-art system. The system combined frontier models, tool use, and safeguards in a multi-stage process to generate reviews for all 22,977 full-review papers in less than a day. A large-scale survey of AAAI-26 authors and program committee members showed that participants not only found AI reviews useful, but actually preferred them to human reviews on key dimensions such as technical accuracy and research suggestions. We also introduce a novel benchmark and find that our system substantially outperforms a simple LLM-generated review baseline at detecting a variety of scientific weaknesses. Together, these results show that state-of-the-art AI methods can already make meaningful contributions to scientific peer review at conference scale, opening a path toward the next generation of synergistic human-AI teaming for evaluating research.
2604.13940.paper_summary_zh: 科學同行評審面臨著越來越大的壓力，因為提交量激增，使得維持評審質量、一致性和及時性變得越來越困難。最近在人工智慧（AI）方面的進展使得社群開始考慮在同行評審中使用它，但一個關鍵的未解決問題是AI是否能在現實世界的會議規模上生成技術上可靠的評審。這裡我們報告了首個大規模的AI輔助同行評審的現場部署：AAAI-26的每一篇主題提交都收到了來自一個最先進系統的清晰標識的AI評審。該系統在一個多階段過程中結合了前沿模型、工具使用和安全措施，在不到一天的時間內為22,977篇完整評審論文生成了評審。對AAAI-26的作者和程序委員會成員進行的大規模調查顯示，參與者不僅認為AI評審有用，實際上在技術準確性和研究建議等關鍵維度上更偏好AI評審而非人類評審。我們還引入了一個新基準，發現我們的系統在檢測各種科學弱點方面顯著優於簡單的LLM生成評審基準。總體而言，這些結果顯示最先進的AI方法已經能夠在會議規模上對科學同行評審做出有意義的貢獻，為下一代協同人類-AI團隊評估研究開辟了一條道路。
2604.13940.author: Joydeep Biswas et.al.
2604.13940.authors: Joydeep Biswas, Sheila Schoepp, Gautham Vasan, Anthony Opipari, Arthur Zhang, Zichao Hu, Sebastian Joseph, Matthew Lease, Junyi Jessy Li, Peter Stone, Kiri L. Wagstaff, Matthew E. Taylor, Odest Chadwicke Jenkins
